name: NUTCRACKER PHENOMENON
definition: RENAL VEIN ENTRAPMENT SYNDROME entrapment of left renal vein (LRV) between SMA and aorta (= anterior nutcracker) / between
dominant_finding: venous flow velocity of > 100 cm/s (78% sensitive, 100% specific)
distribution: compression of left renal vein + prestenotic dilatation
discriminating_features: venous flow velocity of > 100 cm/s (78% sensitive, 100% specific)
compression of left renal vein + prestenotic dilatation
reflux into dilated adrenal + gonadal veins
hilar + periureteric + perirenal + pelvic venous collaterals

general: compression of left renal vein + prestenotic dilatation
reflux into dilated adrenal + gonadal veins
hilar + periureteric + perirenal + pelvic venous collaterals
US: venous flow velocity of > 100 cm/s (78% sensitive, 100% specific)
peak systolic velocity ratio of > 4.7 at point of renal vein compression compared to hilar
indentations / scalloping of renal pelvis / ureter ← enlarged retroperitoneal varices
renal venous-caval pressure gradient > 3 mmHg (less than 1 mmHg in healthy subjects)
